channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: La deuda estudiantil les agobiaba, así que se mudaron al extranjero y dejaron de pagar
item.description: Un número récord de prestatarios de préstamos estudiantiles se encuentran en situación de morosidad e impago. Algunos están tomando la drástica decisión de irse del país y abandonar sus préstamos.
item.dc:creator: Laura O’Connor

media:content.media:credit: Milan Bures para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Amanda Lynn Tully, de 37 años, se mudó a Praga y dejó de pagar sus préstamos estudiantiles. Lleva más de siete años sin pagar nada.

item.title: Sharpton Moves Longtime Civil Rights Group to New Home in Harlem
item.description: The Rev. Al Sharpton wants to leave his mark in the face of gentrification, which he says has diluted Harlem’s political power.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Latson

media:content.media:credit: Elias Williams for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Rev. Al Sharpton said his civil rights organization, the National Action Network, was buying the Faison Firehouse Theater in Harlem to be its new home.

item.title: Someone Changed His Party Registration. He Thinks He Knows Why.
item.description: Andrew Hevesi, a lifelong Democrat and New York State assemblyman from Queens, was told his voter registration had been changed without his knowledge. He has a culprit in mind.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford

media:content.media:credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Andrew Hevesi, a New York State assemblyman from Queens, says his rival in the Democratic primary had a motive to file paperwork to change Mr. Hevesi’s party registration.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 6, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Oil Rises Slightly After Trump’s Latest Threats on Iran
item.description: President Trump taunted Iranian leaders on Sunday, threatening to escalate attacks if the Strait of Hormuz was not reopened.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: The War Is Turning Iran Into a Major World Power
item.description: Its newfound might derives from its control the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Robert A. Pape
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Tam Stockton for The New York Times

item.title: ‘Euphoria,’ Plus 8 Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: The HBO show returns after a long hiatus. And the March Madness final airs.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Dulany

media:content.media:description: Zendaya returns as Rue in Season 3 of “Euphoria.”

item.title: Los mayores visten bien para afrontar el horror de la guerra
item.description: En Ucrania, mucha gente mayor se viste con elegancia ante el horror. Muestran su determinación de seguir adelante a pesar de la guerra.
item.dc:creator: Alyona Synenko and Olga Engibarova

media:content.media:description: Anna Rozen

item.title: Artemis II Moon Flyby: Crew, Timeline and What to Know
item.description: The journey around the moon of three Americans and one Canadian is going into its sixth day, but it’s not too late to get caught up on it.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang

media:content.media:description: The SLS rocket taking off from Launch Complex 39B to begin the Artemis II mission last Wednesday in Cape Canaveral, Fla.

item.title: This Is Not China’s War, but Beijing Started Preparing for It Years Ago
item.description: Long concerned about geopolitical crises, China redoubled efforts to secure energy security when President Trump started raising the stakes in his first term.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Murphy Zhao
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Oil storage tanks and facilities of a Sinopec plant in Shanghai last month.

item.title: China Mourned an Education Influencer. The Grief Was a Quiet Revolt.
item.description: Zhang Xuefeng helped people navigate the country’s unforgiving higher education system. The public outpouring after his death was a quiet rebuke to the punishing process.
item.dc:creator: Li Yuan

media:content.media:description: Mourners lined up in Suzhou, China, to pay their respects to Zhang Xuefeng, a prominent education influencer who died on March 24.

item.title: Quote of the Day: First to Be Disrupted By A.I.? Its Creators.
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, April 6, 2026.

item.title: ‘The Comeback’ Season 3, Episode 3: The Aww-Ick
item.description: This week’s episode suggests that Lisa Kudrow and Michael Patrick King don’t mind mixing in a little sentiment with their satire.
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray

media:content.media:description: Lisa Kudrow in “The Comeback.”

item.title: NASA Artemis II Astronauts Race Into Moon’s Embrace After Quiet Easter
item.description: Ahead of a lunar flyby on Monday, the crew celebrated the astronaut Jeremy Hansen’s first spaceflight and got a special message from Charlie Duke, the Apollo 16 moonwalker.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang

media:content.media:description: A view of the near side of the moon, the side we always see from Earth, captured by the Artemis II astronauts on Sunday.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Sunday
item.description: President Trump taunted Iran on social media, while strikes continued in Iran, Israel, Lebanon and some Gulf states.
item.dc:creator: Eve Sampson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Emergency medical workers search through the rubble of a residential building struck by an Iranian missile attack on Sunday in Haifa, Israel.

item.title: 10-Minute Challenge: ‘I Spy’
item.description: We’d like you to look at one piece of art for 10 minutes, uninterrupted.
item.dc:creator: Larry Buchanan

item.title: U.S. Military Rescues Airman Stranded in Iran
item.description: An Air Force officer whose fighter jet was shot down in Iran was rescued by U.S. Special Operations forces in a risky Saturday night mission that took commandos deep into enemy territory, President Trump said on social media early on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Personal de la Fuerza Aérea estadounidense el mes pasado en una base aérea en el Reino Unido.

item.title: Trump’s Lesson From Risky Rescue: Threaten to Go Harder at Iran
item.description: In an expletive-laced social media post, the president said Iran should open the Strait of Hormuz or he would bomb bridges and power plants.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: While President Trump still suggests that Iran is begging for a deal, intelligence analysis generated by the United States and some of its closest Western allies suggests the opposite.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 6, 2026
item.description: Log on for a new puzzle from John Ewbank.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher

media:content.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: It’s me, hi, I’m the puzzle, it’s me.

item.title: ICE Agents Detain Newlywed Spouse of Soldier Training to Deploy
item.description: The 22-year-old wife of an Army staff sergeant came to the U.S. as a toddler. She was taken from a military base where the couple planned to live.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Lily Brooks for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Staff Sergeant Matthew Blank and his mother, Jen Rickling, outside the detention center in Basile, La., where his wife is being held.

item.title: A Looming Deadline
item.description: We look at the options for reopening the Strait of Hormuz as President Trump escalates his threats.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: A Food Pantry Network Suddenly Shuttered, Leaving Thousands Scrambling
item.description: Ruby’s Pantry had 85 locations in communities in Minnesota, Wisconsin, North Dakota and Iowa.
item.dc:creator: Sonia A. Rao and Bernard Mokam

media:content.media:description: As the cost of groceries has surged, federal food assistance programs around the country have been threatened.

item.title: 3 Dead After High Winds Topple Tree During Easter Egg Hunt in Germany
item.description: A 21-year-old woman, her baby and a 16-year-old girl were killed after a nearly 100-foot tree fell in a wooded area in northern Germany, the police said.
item.dc:creator: Livia Albeck-Ripka

media:content.media:description: The three people killed were among a group of residents and staff members from a nearby welfare facility who had been searching for Easter eggs in a wooded area, the police said.

item.title: NYC Easter Parade and Bonnet Festival Brightens a Gray Sunday on Fifth Avenue: Photos
item.description: April showers brought silk flowers on Sunday as New Yorkers promenaded along Fifth Avenue in Manhattan for the annual Easter Parade and Bonnet Festival.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: Democrats, Republicans Clash Over Iran War in Week 6
item.description: A swing-district Republican called the war an “incredible operation,” and President Trump drew backlash from political figures in both parties over a social media post.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Here’s a Timeline of Trump’s Ultimatums Over the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump first gave Iran 48 hours to reopen the vital oil shipping route on March 21. The deadline has been reset many times since.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries

media:content.media:description: Boats in the Strait of Hormuz in March. Transit through the waterway has slowed to a trickle since the start of the conflict.

item.title: A Harrowing Race Against Time to Find a Downed U.S. Airman in Iran
item.description: For the Iranians, the Air Force colonel whose fighter jet had been shot down was possible leverage. For the U.S. military, finding him was a moral imperative.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe, Helene Cooper, Eric Schmitt and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: An image shared by Iranian state television on social media purported to show the site of the U.S. aircraft destroyed during the mission to find a stranded airman in Iran.

item.title: Muere el torero retirado Ricardo Ortiz embestido por un toro en Málaga
item.description: El accidente ocurrió antes de la Corrida Picassiana, un evento anual en Málaga que rinde homenaje al pintor Pablo Picasso.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses

media:content.media:description: Un torero realizando un pase durante la Corrida Picassiana en Málaga en 2024.

item.title: Fuerzas especiales de EE. UU. rescatan a un tripulante del avión derribado en Irán
item.description: Un oficial de la Fuerza Aérea de un F-15E Strike Eagle derribado el viernes por Irán pasó un día en territorio hostil con poco más que una pistola como protección.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper, Greg Jaffe and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Personal de la Fuerza Aérea estadounidense el mes pasado en una base aérea en el Reino Unido.

item.title: Trump Revels in Threats to Commit War Crimes in Iran
item.description: The president said he would bomb Iran “back to the Stone Ages.” Until this administration, American leaders had insisted they were trying to follow international law in war.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump wrote on social media that “all Hell will reign down” on Iran unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Decenas de muertos en una masacre en Haití mientras llega la fuerza internacional
item.description: Las pandillas arrasaron varias comunidades el pasado fin de semana, lo que destaca los retos a los que se enfrentará la nueva Fuerza de Supresión de Pandillas, respaldada por la ONU.
item.dc:creator: André Paultre and Frances Robles

item.title: Pepsi Drops Sponsorship of Wireless Festival Headlined by Kanye West
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he was “deeply concerned” that the rapper known for antisemitic and racist comments had been booked to perform at the Wireless Festival.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear

media:content.media:description: Kanye West in Milan in 2024.

item.title: OPEC Plus Warns of Slow Recovery After War in Iran
item.description: The group of influential oil exporters also said it was increasing its production quotas, a largely symbolic move.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries

media:content.media:description: Smoke rising from an oil warehouse following a suspected drone attack last week near Erbil, Iraq.

item.title: More Than 70 Migrants Missing After Boat Capsizes Off Libya
item.description: Survivors said at least 100 people were on a boat that had left Libya for Europe. Only 32 of those aboard are known to have survived.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo

media:content.media:credit: Fabian Melber/Sea-Watch, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A photograph released by the nongovernmental organization Sea-Watch showing migrants on a capsized boat off Libya on Saturday.

item.title: Simple Bread Pudding, Spring Vegetables Every Which Way and Hot Cross Buns
item.description: Happy Easter, happy spring and happy Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Ko

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Testani for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Simon Andrews.
media:content.media:description: Mark Bittman’s simple bread pudding.

item.title: How the CIA Helped Locate a Missing Airman After Iran Downed a U.S. Fighter Jet
item.description: The intelligence agency also executed a deception campaign aimed at drawing Iranian forces away from where the missing Air Force officer was hiding.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A still image from a video of U.S. aircraft over southwest Iran on Friday.

item.title: Former Matador Is Gored to Death Before Annual Bullfight in Spain
item.description: The accident happened before the Corrida Picassiana, an annual event in Malaga that honors the painter Pablo Picasso.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses

media:content.media:description: A matador performing a pass during the Corrida Picassiana in Malaga in 2024.

item.title: Trump Seems Emboldened With New Threats in Truth Social Post Over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: In an expletive-filled social media post, Mr. Trump said Iran should open the Strait of Hormuz or he will bomb bridges and power plants.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

media:content.media:description: President Trump at the White House last week.

item.title: On ‘S.N.L.,’ Bondi Boasts of Being ‘First Woman Ever Fired as Attorney General’
item.description: Ashley Padilla was both Pam Bondi and Kristi Noem on “Saturday Night Live,” with Tina Fey, Candice Bergen and Melissa McCarthy. Jack Black and Jack White were host and musical guest.
item.dc:creator: Dave Itzkoff

item.title: Iran Is Ending the Dream of Remote-Controlled War
item.description: A.I.’s significant targeting improvements aren’t enough to overcome geography in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Marc Gustafson and Justin Kosslyn

item.title: Not Just for Rock Stars: Playing in the Band
item.description: Readers respond to “Change Your Life. Start a Band,” an Opinion guest essay by Hugo Lindgren. Also: Bruce Springsteen’s art.

item.title: Iran’s Downing of Fighter Jet and U.S. Rescue Leave Both Sides Dangerously Emboldened
item.description: After Iran shot down a U.S. plane and U.S. forces pulled off a risky ground operation to extricate a stranded airman, both sides claimed victory. That confidence could fuel further escalation.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An anti-U.S. billboard on Sunday in Tehran showing American aircraft captured in a net.

item.title: Is Christianity Making a Comeback?
item.description: Some see Easter Sunday’s packed pews and overflowing chapels as a sign of a religious revival. But experts are urging caution.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Jackson

media:content.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A Mass in Chicago in 2025.

item.title: Easter, Passover, and Artemis II
item.description: The holidays and the moon launch each tell a redemption story. Why do those stories keep appearing, again and again?
item.dc:creator: Lauren Jackson

media:content.media:credit: Cassandra Klos for The New York Times

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Calls for Peace in First Easter Mass
item.description: The pontiff’s Easter remarks follow a Palm Sunday homily in which he said God rejected the prayers of “those who wage war.”
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV arriving on Sunday in St. Peter’s Square for Easter Mass, during which he renewed his pleas for peace and condemned violence.

item.title: The Light That Changed My Life
item.description: The light that changed my life.
item.dc:creator: David French

item.title: Change Doesn’t Happen the Way You Think
item.description: The self-transformation industry sells control. Real change is messier.
item.dc:creator: Benoit Denizet-Lewis

item.title: The Old Way of Campaigning Won’t Cut It Anymore
item.description: Is this a great way to audition and select our leaders, especially for executive offices? Not particularly.
item.dc:creator: David Plouffe

item.title: The Daily: A Renowned Opera Singer Risked Her Voice for Motherhood
item.description: For the soprano Lise Davidsen, parenthood has turned her relationship to work – and to her art – into an open question.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Kitroeff, Tina Antolini, Luke Vander Ploeg, Alex Barron, Zachary Woolfe, Wendy Dorr, Sophia Lanman, Dan Powell and Diane Wong

media:content.media:credit: Amir Hamja for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lise Davidsen, 39, said her return to the stage has been immensely challenging emotionally.
